Value: Rubber-residue contamination arises from vulcanized rubber deposits of tire marks, seals, and gaskets, so it forms during friction and compression on surfaces. This residue shows unique elasticity and strong adhesion that distinguish it from oily or powdery contaminants because of its polymeric nature. On materials like steel or concrete, residue clings tightly and resists scraping, so removal challenges include incomplete cleaning without damaging substrate, yet laser cleaning succeeds by vaporizing layers precisely during pulsed exposure.
